parameters.prompt:
Du bist ein Daten-Extraktor für Dee Empire. Nutze ZWINGEND das Tool fetch_website, um den Inhalt dieser URL zu lesen: {{node_loop.item.url}}.
Extrahiere aus dem Seiteninhalt ein reines JSON-Objekt (kein Markdown, keine Backticks, nur JSON):
{"company_name": "...", "website_url": "...", "phone": "...", "email": "...", "icebreaker": "..."}
- website_url: die echte URL (nutze {{node_loop.item.url}} falls unklar).
- phone/email: echte Kontaktdaten von der Seite; wenn nicht auffindbar, 'N/A'.
- icebreaker: EIN kurzer, persönlicher deutscher Eröffnungssatz (max. 25 Wörter), der sich konkret auf etwas von der Website bezieht.
Wenn die Seite nicht geladen werden kann, gib trotzdem das JSON mit 'N/A' für unbekannte Felder zurück. Gib NUR das JSON aus, sonst nichts.

parameters.query: INSERT INTO leads (company_name, website_url, phone, email, notes, status, created_at, updated_at) VALUES ('{{node_parse_json.output.company_name}}', NULLIF(NULLIF('{{node_parse_json.output.website_url}}','N/A'),''), NULLIF(NULLIF('{{node_parse_json.output.phone}}','N/A'),''), NULLIF(NULLIF('{{node_parse_json.output.email}}','N/A'),''), NULLIF(NULLIF('{{node_parse_json.output.icebreaker}}','N/A'),''), 'new', NOW(), NOW()) ON CONFLICT (website_url) DO NOTHING

parameters.noteText:
DATA SCRAPING TEMPLATE - OPERATOR OVERVIEW

Purpose: find real local businesses from the editable campaign prompt, extract usable lead data, insert new leads into the leads table, and email an internal run report.

This template is meant to be reused by changing only EDIT CAMPAIGN PROMPT -> campaign_prompt, max_searches_per_run, and max_leads_per_run. Do not change the graph, database queries, loop settings, extraction scripts, or email report unless you are intentionally changing workflow mechanics.

Safe operating rule: the campaign prompt controls what to search for. The workflow mechanics control how leads are parsed, deduplicated, analyzed, inserted, and reported. Keep those responsibilities separate.

Final normal output: Run Summary -> Fetch Leads Collected This Run -> Build Email Report -> Email Lead Report.

PHASE 1 - EDITABLE CAMPAIGN PROMPT AND SEARCH BRIEF

Nodes in this phase:
1. Trigger: Webhook
2. EDIT CAMPAIGN PROMPT
3. Build Prompt Search Brief
4. Gemini 2.5 Flash
5. Google Search
6. Search Agent

What happens:
- Webhook starts the run.
- EDIT CAMPAIGN PROMPT provides the campaign instructions.
- Build Prompt Search Brief reads campaign_prompt, max_searches_per_run, and max_leads_per_run.
- It also loads recent existing lead domains from the database so the Search Agent can avoid them.
- Search Agent creates/searches Google queries from the campaign prompt and returns lead candidates as JSON.

What the user should edit:
- campaign_prompt: towns, business types, lead signals, exclusions, target result count.
- max_searches_per_run: search query limit.
- max_leads_per_run: how many leads to queue.

Debug when broken:
- If no leads are found, inspect campaign_prompt first.
- If results are wrong, tighten towns, business types, and exclusions.
- If old leads repeat, inspect excluded_domains_text from Build Prompt Search Brief.
- If the prompt is empty, Search Agent should return {"leads":[]}.

PHASE 2 - EXTRACT LEADS, LOOP, AND DEDUP GATE

Nodes in this phase:
1. Extract Leads
2. Loop Leads
3. Check DB for Lead
4. Lead Exists?
5. Log: Skipped Existing Lead
6. Run Summary

What happens:
- Extract Leads normalizes Search Agent output into an items array for the loop.
- It filters blocked domains and bad lead text, removes duplicate URLs, and does a batch duplicate check by website_url, phone, and company_name.
- Loop Leads processes one candidate at a time.
- Check DB for Lead is currently a pass-through query that returns no existing row. Duplicate handling is mainly done inside Extract Leads and final insert protection.
- Lead Exists? routes false to Analyze Agent and true to skip log. In this template the condition is intentionally False so queued items continue to analysis.
- Run Summary fires when the loop completes.

Important behavior:
- This template targets businesses with real URLs/contact pages. If the campaign is for businesses with no website, the search prompt must still provide a source URL that can be analyzed.
- Existing leads should be reduced before the loop by Extract Leads.
- Insert Lead still uses ON CONFLICT as the final database safety net.

Debug when broken:
- If everything is skipped, inspect Extract Leads total_new and duplicate_status.
- If the loop receives no items, inspect Search Agent output and Extract Leads output.
- If Lead Exists? blocks unexpectedly, inspect its condition and incoming DB output.
- If candidates are junk, update BLOCKED_DOMAINS/BAD_LEAD_RE only if the problem is repeatable.

PHASE 3 - ANALYZE WEBSITE, PARSE CONTACT DATA, INSERT LEAD

Nodes in this phase:
1. Gemini 2.5 Pro
2. Fetch Website
3. Analyze Agent
4. Parse Agent JSON
5. Insert Lead
6. End Iteration

What happens:
- Analyze Agent receives the current loop item URL.
- It must use Fetch Website to read the page.
- It extracts company_name, website_url, phone, email, and icebreaker as JSON.
- Parse Agent JSON cleans the AI output and escapes values for SQL.
- Insert Lead writes into leads with status new.
- ON CONFLICT (website_url) DO NOTHING prevents duplicate website inserts.
- End Iteration merges inserted and skipped paths so the loop can continue cleanly.

What must be true:
- node_loop.item.url should be a usable URL.
- Analyze Agent must return JSON only, no markdown.
- leads table must support the inserted fields.

Debug when broken:
- If inserted rows have Unknown/N/A, inspect Analyze Agent output and fetched website content.
- If SQL fails, inspect Parse Agent JSON escaping and Insert Lead query.
- If no row is inserted but no error occurs, ON CONFLICT probably found an existing website_url.
- If Fetch Website fails, Analyze Agent should still return JSON with N/A for unknown fields.

nodes.name: EDIT CAMPAIGN PROMPT
nodes.description: Template control node. Edit only campaign_prompt to change town, niche, search target, exclusions, and desired lead type.

fields.name: campaign_prompt
fields.value:
Goal:
Find local car service businesses, mechanics, and auto repair companies that may need a better website, online appointment booking system, customer inquiry form, service request workflow, or automated customer support from Dee Empire.

Target locations:
- Lichtenfels
- Coburg
- Kronach
- Kulmbach
- Bamberg
- Bayreuth
- Altenkunstadt
- Burgkunstadt
- Bad Staffelstein
- Nearby Oberfranken towns only if the exact towns have too few results

Business types:
- KFZ Werkstatt
- Autowerkstatt
- Car repair shop
- Auto service center
- Reifenservice / tire service
- Auto detailing
- Car wash / Autowäsche
- Fahrzeugaufbereitung
- TÜV service providers
- Car body repair / Karosseriebau
- Paint repair / Lackiererei
- Used car dealers with repair service
- Motorcycle repair shops
- Truck repair services
- Mobile mechanics

Good lead signals:
- No visible online appointment booking
- Website looks old, simple, slow, or not mobile-friendly
- Contact is mainly phone, email, or a basic contact form
- Services are listed but customers cannot request a service online
- No clear pricing, service packages, or booking process
- No chatbot or automated customer support visible
- No online form for repair requests, inspection requests, tire change, or TÜV appointment
- Website is only one page or missing clear service pages
- Business appears active but has weak digital presence
- Local independent business, not a big franchise or national chain
- Good fit for appointment booking, customer reminders, CRM follow-up, and website modernization

Exclude:
- Google Maps
- Facebook
- Instagram
- LinkedIn
- TikTok
- YouTube
- Mobile.de
- AutoScout24
- eBay Kleinanzeigen
- Kleinanzeigen
- Werkstatt portals
- Tire booking platforms
- Check24
- Trustpilot
- Gelbe Seiten
- Das Örtliche
- Yelp
- Meinestadt
- Directories
- Marketplaces
- Newspaper articles
- Government pages
- Chamber of commerce pages
- Franchise chains
- Large national chains

Return:
Real businesses only. Prefer official company websites, contact pages, service pages, booking pages, or inquiry forms. Collect up to 25 unique leads.

nodes.name: Build Prompt Search Brief
nodes.description: Reads the editable campaign prompt and prepares exclusion/domain context for the search agent.

parameters.query: SELECT id FROM leads WHERE false LIMIT 1

nodes.name: End Iteration
nodes.description: Normal path branch merge so the report gate runs after each lead is skipped or inserted.

nodes.name: Fetch Leads Collected This Run
nodes.description: Fetches leads inserted since this workflow execution started.

parameters.query: SELECT company_name, website_url, phone, email, status, notes, created_at FROM leads WHERE created_at >= '{{node_search_brief.run_started_at}}'::timestamptz ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 300

nodes.name: Build Email Report
nodes.description: Builds a Dee Empire HTML report of leads collected in this run.

nodes.name: Email Lead Report
nodes.description: Sends the run lead report. Requires Email Provider in Project Settings.

parameters.noteText:
PHASE 4 - RUN SUMMARY AND EMAIL REPORT

Nodes in this phase:
1. Run Summary
2. Fetch Leads Collected This Run
3. Build Email Report
4. Email Lead Report

What happens:
- Run Summary exposes counts from Extract Leads: leads_found, leads_new, leads_skipped_existing.
- Fetch Leads Collected This Run loads leads created since Build Prompt Search Brief recorded run_started_at.
- Build Email Report creates a Dee Empire HTML report with lead details, contact data, status, and notes/icebreaker.
- Email Lead Report sends the internal report to msesay@dee-empire.com.

Important behavior:
- The email report is based on database rows inserted during this run, not just AI memory.
- If ON CONFLICT prevents all inserts, the report may show zero new leads even if candidates were found.
- This report fires after the loop completes, not after every single lead.

Debug when broken:
- If no email arrives, check Project Settings email provider and Email Lead Report node output.
- If email says zero leads, inspect Insert Lead outputs and Fetch Leads Collected This Run query.
- If counts look wrong, inspect Extract Leads totals.
- If old leads appear in email, check run_started_at from Build Prompt Search Brief.
